focus: Adversarial — verdict challenge HYBRID vs new skill

description: HYBRID approach requires operators to search across 5+ skills (cli-devin, cli-opencode, sk-prompt, sk-code, mcp-code-mode) to find small-model patterns, creating cognitive load and 'where is the pattern?' search problem. A dedicated sk-small-model skill would provide a single canonical source.
recommended_fix: Create sentinel sk-small-model skill with pattern-index.md that maps all distributed pattern locations, providing single entry point while keeping patterns distributed where used

description: HYBRID approach requires 5 enhances edges (cli-devin↔cli-opencode at 0.5, cli-devin→sk-prompt at 0.4, sk-code→cli-devin at 0.4, cli-devin→mcp-code-mode at 0.3) plus 50+ trigger_phrases across 4 skills to achieve advisor routing. A dedicated skill would require only 1 skill entry with 10-15 trigger_phrases.
recommended_fix: Sentinel sk-small-model skill provides single routing target with 10-15 trigger_phrases; enhances edges from sentinel to cli-devin/cli-opencode replace complex multi-skill wiring

description: User's stated goal from spec.md §2 Purpose is 'when you call cli-devin it will also automatically prompt to check skill that has logic, workflows etc for smaller models'. HYBRID fails this goal because it scatters small-model logic across existing CLI skills without a single 'check this skill' signal.
recommended_fix: Sentinel sk-small-model skill provides explicit 'check this skill' signal that maps directly to user's mental model; advisor surfaces sentinel skill on any small-model dispatch

description: HYBRID approach requires maintaining 41 artifacts across 7 target locations (cli-devin/references/, cli-opencode/references/, cli-devin/assets/, cli-opencode/assets/, AGENTS.md, 4 graph-metadata.json files). A dedicated skill would centralize all small-model artifacts in one place.
recommended_fix: HYBRID-with-Anchor compromise: sentinel skill holds minimal state (enhances edges + AGENTS.md pointer + pattern index); patterns stay distributed where used to preserve CLI-specific integration

description: HYBRID rationale conflates implementation details with architectural boundaries. A dedicated sk-small-model skill could provide model-agnostic patterns (budget formulas, verification stages, escalation matrices) while delegating CLI-specific integration to consuming skills via clear contracts, similar to sk-prompt precedent.
recommended_fix: Sentinel skill provides model-agnostic pattern philosophy; CLI-specific integration contracts remain in consuming skills per HYBRID approach

description: HYBRID relies on graph_causal lane boosts from enhances edges as secondary signal. A dedicated skill would have strong lexical/semantic signals from single skill entry, producing higher advisor scores for small-model prompts.
recommended_fix: Sentinel skill provides strong lexical/semantic signals; enhances edges from sentinel provide co-surfacing of distributed patterns

description: Three patterns work best as unified skill: (1) Model Profile Registry (8 models with context windows, tool calling support), (2) Escalation Decision Matrix (when_to_downgrade, when_to_escalate, quota_aware rules), (3) Bayesian Tool Scoring with Laplace Smoothing. Distributed across cli-devin/assets/ and cli-opencode/assets/, these risk divergence.
recommended_fix: Sentinel skill's pattern-index.md references centralized model-profiles.json and escalation-providers.json in cli-devin/assets/ as shared assets; Bayesian tool scoring documented in sentinel skill references/ with implementation guidance per CLI skill

description: HYBRID rationale correctly identifies that small-model patterns are CLI-specific with different runtime defaults (cli-devin needs SWE-1.6 budget defaults, cli-opencode needs DeepSeek permissions matrix). A dedicated skill would either duplicate CLI-specific logic or provide only abstract patterns lacking concrete integration.
recommended_fix: HYBRID-with-Anchor preserves CLI-specific integration: patterns stay distributed in cli-devin/references/ and cli-opencode/references/ with concrete contracts; sentinel skill provides only philosophical guidance and index

description: sk-prompt precedent (enhances edges to all CLI skills with weight 0.4) does not apply because sk-prompt is framework-agnostic (same cli_prompt_quality_card.md to all skills), whereas small-model patterns are runtime-environment-specific (different defaults per model and CLI orchestrator).
recommended_fix: Sentinel skill acknowledges this distinction: provides model-agnostic philosophy but defers CLI-specific implementation to distributed patterns

description: HYBRID's 5-lane scoring simulation (iter-010) confirms all 3 sample prompts clear 0.8 threshold with proposed enhances edges + trigger_phrases. A dedicated skill would not materially improve routing — HYBRID already achieves automatic routing.
recommended_fix: Sentinel skill simplifies routing (single skill entry vs complex multi-skill wiring) while maintaining automatic routing capability

description: HYBRID's maintenance cost is actually LOWER because patterns live where they're used. A dedicated skill would create tight coupling: every CLI skill integration contract change would require corresponding updates to the dedicated skill.
recommended_fix: HYBRID-with-Anchor preserves HYBRID's maintenance advantage: sentinel skill holds minimal state, no tight coupling to consuming skills

description: User-goal mapping shows: DEDICATED skill best satisfies 'check skill' goal (HIGH), HYBRID satisfies indirectly via AGENTS.md rule (MEDIUM-HIGH), HYBRID-with-Anchor provides middle ground (MEDIUM-HIGH with single routing target).
recommended_fix: Adopt HYBRID-with-Anchor to satisfy user goal while preserving HYBRID's maintenance advantages

description: Final verdict: HYBRID-with-Anchor. Create sentinel sk-small-model skill with enhances edges + AGENTS.md rule pointer + pattern index, while keeping all actual patterns distributed. This satisfies user goal (single 'check skill' signal) while preserving HYBRID's maintenance advantages (patterns live where used).
recommended_fix: Implement sentinel skill structure: SKILL.md (1-2 paragraph philosophy + AGENTS.md pointer), graph-metadata.json (enhances edges to cli-devin at 0.5, cli-opencode at 0.5), references/pattern-index.md (index of all distributed patterns with file paths)
